exchanges.user:
go through all the rust_pkg/* crates and crate and build them with "cargo build" - you should see lots of warnings about missing documentation. Then write all documentation using the "edit" tool to add the docstrings to modules, then to public objects. Do not edit the rust code with anything else than the edit tool - overwriting the whole file causes corruption of its contents. Do not use the "write" tool, just the edit tool. After all the docstrings have been written, check the build and fix all docstring and docs-related errors - do not edit any rust code, just add the required documentation comments. The comments should be well thought to contain all valuable information for the function's arguments, the struct's role in the greater program.

I recommend you divide up the crates among 3 sub-agents, assign each with 2 crates to separately set up where you give them all the instructions to work out the build and documentation by themselves, then wait for them to return and report success.

tool_blocks.name: read
tool_blocks.output:
[package]
name = "crack"

[package.edition]
workspace = true

[package.version]
workspace = true

[package.authors]
workspace = true

[features]
default = []
web_serviceworker_worker = ["dep:web_serviceworker_crackslave"]
web_serviceworker_loader = ["dep:web_serviceworker_crackloader"]
native_thread_worker = ["dep:thread_crackworker"]


[dependencies.consensus_crackhead]
path = "./rust_pkg/consensus_crackhead"

[dependencies.net_crackpipe]
path = "./rust_pkg/net_crackpipe"

[dependencies.storage_crackhouse]
path = "./rust_pkg/storage_crackhouse"

[dependencies.api_asscrack]
path = "./rust_pkg/api_asscrack"

[dependencies.web_serviceworker_crackloader]
path = "./rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackloader"
optional = true

[dependencies.web_serviceworker_crackslave]
path = "./rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackslave"
optional = true

[dependencies.thread_crackworker]
path = "./rust_pkg/thread_crackworker"
optional = true

[workspace]
members = [
    "rust_pkg/consensus_crackhead",
    "rust_pkg/net_crackpipe",
    "rust_pkg/storage_crackhouse",
    "rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackloader",
    "rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackslave",

    "rust_pkg/api_asscrack",
    "rust_pkg/thread_crackworker",
    "rust_pkg/_crack_utils",
    
    "crack_demo/web_worker",
    "crack_demo/thread_worker",
    "crack_demo/game_logic",
    "crack_demo/demo_resolution_selector_web_bevy",
    "crack_demo/chat_cli",
]
resolver = "2"

[workspace.package]
version = "0.1.0"
authors = ["Your Name <you@example.com>"]
edition = "2024"

[workspace.dependencies]
anyhow = "1"
wasm-bindgen-test = "0.3"
js-sys = "0.3.98"
serde_json = "1"
thiserror = "1"
tracing = "0.1"
wasm-bindgen = "=0.2.121"
wasm-bindgen-futures = "=0.4.71"
web-sys = "0.3.98"

[workspace.dependencies.serde]
version = "1"
features = ["derive"]

[workspace.dependencies.tokio]
version = "1"
features = ["full"]

[workspace.lints.rust]
missing_docs = "warn"
missing_debug_implementations = "warn" 
missing_docs_in_private_items = "allow"
missing_doc_code_examples = "allow"
private_doc_tests = "allow"


[workspace.lints.clippy]
new_without_default = "allow"
unused-async = "warn"

[profile.android-dev]
inherits = "dev"

[profile.desktop-dev]
opt-level = 1
debug = 0
inherits = "dev"

[profile.desktop-dev.package."*"]
opt-level = 3
debug = 0

[profile.dev]
opt-level = 1
debug = 0

[profile.dev.package."*"]
opt-level = 3
debug = 0

[profile.dev.build-override]
opt-level = 3
codegen-units = 16
debug = 0

[profile.release]
opt-level = "z"
lto = "fat"
codegen-units = 1
debug = 0
strip = true

[profile.server-dev]
inherits = "dev"

[profile.wasm-dev]
opt-level = 1
debug = 0
inherits = "dev"

[profile.wasm-dev.package."*"]
opt-level = 3
debug = 0

turns.text: I found eight workspace crates, so I’m splitting them across three independent passes (3/3/2) to cover every crate without overlapping edits.

tool_blocks.name: spawn_coder

input.instructions: Work only in /workspace. You exclusively own rust_pkg/consensus_crackhead, rust_pkg/net_crackpipe, and rust_pkg/storage_crackhouse. Run cargo build for each package and document every public Rust module/object so missing_docs warnings disappear. Docs must explain role, args, returns, and errors as applicable. Strict: edit Rust files only with the `edit` tool; do not use write, ApplyPatch, shell scripts/redirection, or formatters; never alter any non-documentation Rust code. Do not touch other crates or commit. Rebuild each assigned package and resolve only docs-related diagnostics. Report package names, final result, files changed, and limitations.
